title: Attosecond X-ray transient absorption in condensed matter
text: A core-state-resolved Bloch model establishing the theoretical basis for attosecond X-ray absorption in solids.
citations: A. Picon, L. Plaja, J. Biegert, “Attosecond x-ray transient absorption in condensed-matter: A core-state-resolved Bloch Model”, N. J. Phys. 21, 043029 (2019).

title: Energy conversion pathways between light, carriers and lattice
text: Attosecond core-level spectroscopy resolves, in real time, how energy flows from light into carriers and then into specific lattice modes of a solid.
citations: T. P. H. Sidiropoulos, N. Di Palo, D. E. Rivas, S. Severino, M. Reduzzi, et al., J. Biegert, “Probing the Energy Conversion Pathways between Light, Carriers, and Lattice in Real Time with Attosecond Core-Level Spectroscopy”, Phys. Rev. X 11, 041060 (2021).

title: Enhanced quantum conductivity and many-body effects
text: Attosecond X-ray spectroscopy of a strongly driven semi-metallic light-matter hybrid, quantifying correlated many-body response beyond a single-particle picture.
citations: T. P. H. Sidiropoulos, N. Di Palo, D. E. Rivas, A. Summers, S. Severino, M. Reduzzi, J. Biegert, “Enhanced quantum conductivity and many-body effects in a semi-metallic light-matter hybrid”, Nature Comm. 14, 7407 (2023).

title: Ultrafast nonlinear response of Dirac fermions in graphene
text: Petahertz-scale optical response in graphene, revealing how Dirac fermions react to a driving field on sub-cycle timescales.
citations: M. Baudisch, A. Marini, J. D. Cox, T. Zhu, F. Silva, S. Teichmann, M. Massicotte, F. Koppens, L. S. Levitov, F. J. García de Abajo, J. Biegert, “Ultrafast nonlinear optical response of Dirac fermions in graphene”, Nature Comm. 9, 1018 (2018).

title: High-harmonic spectroscopy of a high-Tc superconductor
text: High-harmonic spectroscopy distinguishes the electronic phases of YBCO across its quantum phase transition — strong-field optics as a probe of correlated matter.
citations: J. Alcala, U. Bhattacharya, J. Biegert, M. Ciappina, U. Elu, T. Graß, P. T. Grochowski, M. Lewenstein, A. Palau, T. P. H. Sidiropoulos, T. Steinle, I. Tyulnev, “High harmonic spectroscopy of quantum phase transitions in a high-Tc superconductor”, Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci. 119, 2207766119 (2022).

title: Controlling valley polarization through topology
text: Valley polarization normally requires a crystal that lacks inversion symmetry — ruling out bulk, centrosymmetric materials. A polarization-shaped light field breaks that symmetry on its own, steering valley and topology in bulk MoS₂ where it should not have been possible: light shaping topology, not just probing it.
citations: I. Tyulnev, A. Jiménez-Galán, J. Poborska, L. Vamos, R. F. Silva, P. St. J. Russell, F. Tani, O. Smirnova, M. Ivanov, J. Biegert, “Valleytronics in bulk MoS2 by optical control of parity and time symmetries”, Nature 628, 746 (2024).

title: The quantum-optical nature of high-harmonic generation
text: Experimental evidence that harmonic emission carries photon-statistical information beyond the coherent field — the observation underlying harmonic photon-statistics spectroscopy.
citations: D. Theidel, V. Shiriaeva, R. Sondenheimer, M. Froidevaux, V. Severin, A. Merdji-Larue, P. Mosel, S. Fröhlich, K.-A. Weber, M. Kovacev, J. Biegert, H. Merdji, “Evidence of the Quantum Optical Nature of High-Harmonic Generation”, PRX Quantum 5, 040319 (2024).

title: Attosecond angular streaking — the attoclock
text: A route to attosecond-resolution measurement of electron wavepackets that requires no attosecond X-ray pulse at all.
citations: P. Eckle, M. P. Smolarski, P. Schlup, J. Biegert, A. Staudte, M. Schöffler, H. G. Muller, R. Dörner, U. Keller, “Attosecond angular streaking”, Nature Phys. 4, 565 (2008).

title: Imaging aligned polyatomic molecules with LIED
text: Laser-induced electron diffraction applied to aligned polyatomic molecules — self-imaging of molecular structure using the molecule's own electrons.
citations: M. Pullen, B. Wolter, A.-T. Le, M. Baudisch, M. Hemmer, A. Senftleben, C. D. Schröter, J. Ullrich, R. Moshammer, C.-D. Lin, J. Biegert, “Imaging aligned polyatomic molecules with laser-induced electron diffraction”, Nature Commun. 6, 7262 (2015).

title: The first molecular selfie
text: A molecule imaged using its own electrons — laser-induced electron diffraction captured di-ionized acetylene breaking apart, resolving the atoms and their proton motion at 600 attosecond and 7 picometre resolution, at the limit of what physics allows.
citations: B. Wolter, M. G. Pullen, A.-T. Le, M. Baudisch, K. Doblhoff-Dier, et al., J. Biegert, “Ultrafast electron diffraction imaging of bond breaking in di-ionized acetylene”, Science 354, 308 (2016).

title: Electronic and nuclear dynamics of molecular ring opening
text: Attosecond core-level absorption disentangles electronic from nuclear motion as a molecule passes through a conical intersection and opens its ring.
citations: S. Severino, K. M. Ziems, M. Reduzzi, A. Summers, H.-W. Sun, Y.-H. Chien, S. Gräfe, J. Biegert, “Attosecond core-level absorption spectroscopy reveals the electronic and nuclear dynamics of molecular ring opening”, Nature Photon. 18, 731–737 (2024).

title: Quantum path interference in high-harmonic generation
text: Direct observation of interference between the quantum trajectories that generate high harmonics — the electron's alternative paths, made visible.
citations: A. Zaïr, M. Holler, A. Guandalini, F. Schapper, J. Biegert, L. Gallmann, U. Keller, A. Wyatt, A. Monmayrand, I. A. Walmsley, E. Cormier, T. Auguste, J. P. Caumes, P. Salieres, “Quantum path interferences in high-order harmonic generation”, Phys. Rev. Lett. 100, 143902 (2008).

title: Isolated attosecond soft X-ray pulses in the water window
text: Spatio-temporal isolation of a single attosecond burst in the water window, giving element-specific access to the carbon, nitrogen and oxygen edges.
citations: F. Silva, S. Teichmann, S. L. Cousin, J. Biegert, “Spatio-temporal isolation of attosecond soft X-ray pulses in the water window”, Nature Commun. 6, 6611 (2015).

title: 0.5 keV soft X-ray attosecond continua
text: Extending coherent attosecond continua to half a kilo-electronvolt, deep into the water window and beyond.
citations: S. M. Teichmann, F. Silva, S. L. Cousin, M. Hemmer, J. Biegert, “0.5 keV soft X-ray attosecond continua”, Nature Commun. 7, 11493 (2016).

title: Seven-micron ultrafast OPCPA
text: Sub-millijoule pulses at 7 μm, pumped at 2 μm, pushing coherent ultrafast sources into the long-wave infrared.
citations: D. Sanchez, M. Hemmer, M. Baudisch, S. L. Cousin, K. Zawilski, P. Schunemann, O. Chalus, C. Simon-Boisson, J. Biegert, “7-micron, ultrafast, sub-mJ-level mid-IR OPCPA pumped at 2 micron”, Optica 3, 147 (2016).

title: A seven-octave coherent light source
text: A single coherent source spanning seven octaves — from the ultraviolet to the terahertz — brighter over a broad range than a synchrotron, giving one tunable backbone for ultrafast spectroscopy across the spectrum.
citations: U. Elu, L. Maidment, L. Vamos, F. Tani, D. Novoa, M. H. Frosz, V. Badikov, D. Badikov, V. Petrov, P. St. J. Russell, J. Biegert, “Seven-octave high-brightness and carrier-envelope-phase-stable light source”, Nature Photon. 15, 277 (2021).

title: Shortest pulse of light ever generated
text: A 19.2 attosecond soft X-ray pulse — below the atomic unit of time, the interval that defines an electron's orbit in hydrogen, and a demonstration of how far the group's source technology now reaches.
citations: F. Ardana-Lamas, S. L. Cousin, J. Lignieres, J. Biegert, “Brilliant source of 19.2 attosecond soft X-ray pulses below the atomic unit of time.” Ultrafast Science 10, 34133 (2025).
